principles.label: Distribute Wealth During Your Lifetime
principles.summary: The wealthy have a moral obligation to actively distribute their fortunes for the public good while they are still alive, rather than hoarding it until death.
principles.details: Carnegie believed that surplus wealth should be viewed as a trust fund. Leaving it to the state or to heirs represents a failure of duty. Actively administering it bridges the gap between the rich and the poor and brings peace to society.
principles.representative_quote: The man who dies thus rich dies disgraced.

principles.label: Empower Self-Improvement Over Direct Charity
principles.summary: Philanthropy should focus on providing the means for people to improve themselves, rather than giving direct handouts that encourage dependency.
principles.details: Providing infrastructure like libraries, universities, and parks acts as a ladder for those who desire to rise. Direct almsgiving degrades the individual and rewards vice or sloth.
principles.representative_quote: In bestowing charity, the main consideration should be to help those who will help themselves; to provide part of the means by which those who desire to improve may do so

principles.label: Libraries are the Ultimate Philanthropic Investment
principles.summary: Funding free public libraries is the most productive use of philanthropic money because it provides ambitious individuals with the tools to educate themselves.
principles.details: Access to books instills a love of reading, which provides lifelong instruction and enjoyment. This holds far more enduring value for a working person than a temporary financial gift or direct wage increase.
principles.representative_quote: A library outranks any other one thing a community can do to benefit its people. It is a never failing spring in the desert.

principles.label: Watch the Costs and Profits Will Follow
principles.summary: Strictly controlling operational costs down to the penny ensures long-term profitability and resilience against market fluctuations.
principles.details: Profits and prices are cyclical and subject to transient market forces, but savings achieved in the cost of goods are permanent. Operational efficiency positions a company to survive downturns and acquire competitors.
principles.representative_quote: Cut the prices, scoop the market, watch the costs, and the profits will take care of themselves.

principles.label: Organize Smarter People
principles.summary: Success comes not from possessing all technical knowledge yourself, but from the ability to organize and manage experts who know more than you do.
principles.details: Understanding human nature and knowing how to fit gifted managers into an overall system is worth more than the physical assets or machinery of a business.
principles.representative_quote: A success not to be attributed to what I have known or done myself, but to the faculty of knowing and choosing others who did know better than myself.

principles.label: Do Not Spoil Heirs with Wealth
principles.summary: Leaving vast fortunes to children is harmful to their character and acts as a burden rather than a blessing.
principles.details: Inherited wealth ruins work ethic and is usually driven by misguided family pride rather than the actual welfare of the child or the state.
principles.representative_quote: I should as soon leave to my son a curse as the almighty dollar.

principles.label: Inequality and Capital Concentration are Beneficial
principles.summary: Great inequality of wealth and the concentration of capital are essential for the progress of the human race and the refinement of civilization.
principles.details: The accumulation of wealth by capable individuals drives down the cost of commodities, turning former luxuries into necessities. It is a natural evolutionary step from a disorganized state to an organized one.
principles.representative_quote: Much better this great irregularity than universal squalor.

principles.label: Cultivate an Internal Scorecard
principles.summary: The most important judge of your life and actions is your own conscience, which cannot be deceived.
principles.details: Rely on your own internal judgment rather than external validation or the sermons of others to guide moral and business decisions.
principles.representative_quote: The judge within sits in the Supreme Court and can never be cheated hence the grand rule of Life your own reproach alone do fear.

principles.label: Maintain Default Optimism
principles.summary: A sunny disposition and the ability to laugh through life's troubles is incredibly valuable and can be actively cultivated.
principles.details: The mind, like the body, can be moved from the shade into the sunshine. This resilience is worth more than a fortune.
principles.representative_quote: A sunny disposition is worth more than a fortune.

principles.label: Concentrate Capital and Attention
principles.summary: Enormous dividends come from investing deeply in a single business rather than spreading your focus and resources.
principles.details: Put every dollar of your capital and all of your time into one line of business and watch it closely.
principles.representative_quote: My advice to young men would be not only to concentrate their whole time and attention on the one business in life in which they engage, but to put every dollar of their capital into it.

frameworks.label: The Gospel of Wealth (Duty of the Wealthy)
frameworks.summary: A framework for how a successful individual should live and dispose of their accumulated fortune.
frameworks.details:
1. Set an example of modest, unostentatious living, shunning display or extravagance.
2. Provide moderately for the legitimate wants of dependents.
3. Treat all remaining surplus revenues as trust funds.
4. Administer these trust funds during your lifetime to produce the most beneficial results for the community.
frameworks.representative_quote: This, then, is held to be the duty of the man of wealth: To set an example of modest, unostentatious living... and, after doing so, to consider all surplus revenues which come to him simply as trust funds...

frameworks.label: Community-Matched Philanthropy
frameworks.summary: A structured approach to funding public institutions to ensure long-term viability and community investment.
frameworks.details:
1. The wealthy benefactor provides the upfront capital required to build the physical infrastructure (e.g., a library building).
2. Require the local government or community to formally agree to fund ongoing maintenance and operations (often a 10% annual match).
3. Integrate the new institution into the city's existing public property and services.
frameworks.representative_quote: ...provided the community will accept and maintain it as a public institution, as much a part of the city property as its public schools...

frameworks.label: Cost-Centric Market Domination
frameworks.summary: A strategy for operating in a commodity or manufacturing business subject to cyclical prices.
frameworks.details:
1. Cut the prices to scoop the market.
2. Watch the costs strictly down to the penny.
3. Allow the profits to take care of themselves.
frameworks.representative_quote: Cut the prices, scoop the market, watch the costs, and the profits will take care of themselves.

mental_models.label: The Millionaire as Trustee
mental_models.summary: Viewing the wealthy individual not as the absolute owner of their surplus, but as a temporary steward or agent holding funds for the public good.
mental_models.details: The trustee leverages their superior business acumen and organizational experience to distribute wealth more effectively than if it were scattered in trifling amounts among the masses.
mental_models.representative_quote: ...the millionaire will be but a trustee for the poor, intrusted for a season with a great part of the increased wealth of the community, but administering it for the community far better than it could or would have done for itself.

mental_models.label: Ladders for the Aspiring (Hand Upward vs Handout)
mental_models.summary: A lens for evaluating philanthropic investments by prioritizing infrastructure that enables ambitious individuals to climb out of poverty.
mental_models.details: Instead of giving direct handouts, wealth should be used to build parks, works of art, and public institutions like libraries that people must actively use to benefit from.
mental_models.representative_quote: Place within the community's reach the ladders upon which the aspiring can rise.

mental_models.label: The Judge Within
mental_models.summary: Viewing one's conscience as an internal Supreme Court that cannot be cheated, serving as the ultimate arbiter of one's actions.
mental_models.details: You evaluate your life story and actions based on your own internal scorecard rather than external validation or public opinion.
mental_models.representative_quote: The judge within sits in the Supreme Court and can never be cheated hence the grand rule of Life your own reproach alone do fear.

mental_models.label: Business as a Giant Machine
mental_models.summary: Treating the complex process of manufacturing and corporate organization as a giant machine where both human and mechanical parts must be constantly retooled and optimized.
mental_models.details: This allows a leader to organize experts and workers to fit into the overall objective without needing to understand the granular technical details of the machinery themselves.

mental_models.label: The Three-Legged Stool of Industry
mental_models.summary: A metaphor viewing the industrial world as an alliance of three equal, interdependent forces: labor, capital, and ability (management).
mental_models.details: If any one leg is missing or unequal, the enterprise cannot stand. Workers, money, and skill are all equally necessary.
mental_models.representative_quote: Labor, capital, and ability are a three-legged stool... They are equal members of the great triple alliance which moves the industrial world.

mental_models.label: The Fishing Sloop (Hub and Spoke Model)
mental_models.summary: A metaphor for system design: a massive central hub is useless to the masses without a network of local branches to distribute its value.
mental_models.details: Carnegie argued against building a grand central library unless it was connected to neighborhood branch systems, ensuring it was accessible to everyday people.
mental_models.representative_quote: A great central public library without branch libraries in a large city resembles a fishing sloop without small boats.

heuristics.label: Help those who will help themselves
heuristics.summary: Direct assistance only to those who demonstrate a genuine desire and effort to improve their own condition.
heuristics.representative_quote: Help those who will help themselves, to provide part of the means by which those who desire to improve may do so.

heuristics.label: Watch costs down to the penny
heuristics.summary: Maintain absolute visibility and strict control over operational expenses at all times.
heuristics.representative_quote: If you know your costs down to the penny, you are always on firm ground.

heuristics.label: Do not delay, do it now
heuristics.summary: Seize opportunities immediately before circumstances change or competitors act.
heuristics.representative_quote: Do not delay. Do it now.

heuristics.label: Judge by actions, not words
heuristics.summary: Evaluate people based on what they actually do rather than what they claim or promise.
heuristics.representative_quote: As I grow older, I pay less attention to what men say. I just watch what they do.

heuristics.label: Give with warm hands
heuristics.summary: Distribute your wealth purposefully while you are still alive to oversee its impact.

heuristics.label: Put all your eggs in one basket
heuristics.summary: Concentrate your capital and attention entirely on one business and monitor it obsessively.
heuristics.representative_quote: Put all your eggs in one basket and watch that basket.

signature_quotes.label: Dying Rich is a Disgrace
signature_quotes.summary: Carnegie's core belief regarding the hoarding of wealth until death.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: The man who dies thus rich dies disgraced.

signature_quotes.label: The Rule of Charity
signature_quotes.summary: Guiding principle on how to effectively give to others.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: In bestowing charity, the main consideration should be to help those who will help themselves; to provide part of the means by which those who desire to improve may do so; to give those who desire to rise the aids by which they may rise; to assist, but rarely or never to do all.

signature_quotes.label: Cost and Profit Mantra
signature_quotes.summary: A mantra repeated by Carnegie regarding his business strategy.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Cut the prices, scoop the market, watch the costs, and the profits will take care of themselves.

signature_quotes.label: The Curse of Inherited Wealth
signature_quotes.summary: Explaining his belief that inherited wealth spoils heirs.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: I should as soon leave to my son a curse as the almighty dollar.

signature_quotes.label: The Value of Optimism
signature_quotes.summary: Discussing the value of default optimism and the ability to laugh through life's troubles.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: A sunny disposition is worth more than a fortune.

signature_quotes.label: The Danger of Unwise Charity
signature_quotes.summary: Warning against the dangers of indiscriminate charity.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: It were better for mankind that the millions of the rich were thrown into the sea than so spent as to encourage the slothful, the drunken, the unworthy.

signature_quotes.label: Actions Over Words
signature_quotes.representative_quote: As I grow older, I pay less attention to what men say. I just watch what they do.

anti_patterns.label: Hoarding Wealth Until Death
anti_patterns.summary: Dying with a massive, unspent fortune rather than actively administering it during one's lifetime.
anti_patterns.details: It is a disgrace and fails to utilize the wealth to solve societal problems. Leaving vast sums in a will merely implies the giver would have kept the money if they could have taken it with them.

anti_patterns.label: Indiscriminate Charity and Almsgiving
anti_patterns.summary: Providing direct financial handouts or unconditional charity to individuals.
anti_patterns.details: It rewards vice rather than relieving virtue, encouraging the slothful and drunken while doing more injury than good. It fails to encourage self-reliance.

anti_patterns.label: Leaving Massive Fortunes to Heirs
anti_patterns.summary: Bequeathing vast wealth to children or family members.
anti_patterns.details: It burdens the children, ruins their work ethic, and frequently leads to the squandering of the fortune in riotous living rather than using it for the public good.

anti_patterns.label: Operating Without Knowing Exact Costs
anti_patterns.summary: Running a business without granular visibility into operational expenses.
anti_patterns.details: It leaves manufacturers in total ignorance of results until the end of the year, acting like 'moles burrowing in the dark,' unable to tell if they are making a profit or a loss.

anti_patterns.label: Ignoring Technological Revolutions
anti_patterns.summary: Failing to recognize and adopt impending technological advancements.
anti_patterns.details: Slower moving competitors get run over by new technology, rendering their old systems, skills, and assets completely worthless.

anti_patterns.label: Binding Trustees to Rigid Causes
anti_patterns.summary: Forcing philanthropic trustees to adhere to strict, perpetual mandates.
anti_patterns.details: Earthly conditions inevitably change, making rigid mandates obsolete or ineffective over time. Trustees must be allowed to use their own judgment to adapt to new realities.
